### Create your branch{.unnumbered}

For each practical session, the teacher provides a starting branch: `tpN-start`.

You will have to create a personal branch: `<username>-tpN` (e.g. *camille-tp2*) and work on your own branch.

To start with a clean and up-to-date codebase:

- [ ] `git fetch origin` to retrieve the latest remote information  
  - Use this if the `tpN-start` branch was updated after you cloned the repository
- [ ] Create (and switch to) your branch: `git switch -c <username>-tp2 origin/tp2-start`
- [ ] `git push -u origin <username>-tp2` to publish your branch on the remote

Then use the standard Git cycle: add, commit, push after each part.
